_comment_source: Scaffolded from tdd.md (APPROVED, 2026-07-05) by the scaffolder agent.
Single-agent, voice-only (GTP telephony), Slot-Filling DAG Framework for FNOL capture.
See tdd.md Architecture/Tools/Variables/Callbacks sections for full design rationale.
name: insurance_claims
description: CGU express claim lodgement (FNOL) voice agent. Persona 'Amanda'. Captures First Notice of Loss data via a slot-filling framework, guards against active emergencies, and declines out-of-scope requests.
_comment_model_settings: Voice modality (GTP telephony) requires the '-live' streaming model per tdd.md Architecture.
Stakeholder confirmed default platform speaking rate/voice persona -- no speakingRate override (tdd.md §2).
_comment_logging_settings: Voice/audio agent -- evaluationAudioRecordingConfig is required for golden runs.
gcsBucket taken from gecx-config.json's gcs_bucket field.
_comment_variables: telephony-caller-id: GTP-supplied session parameter (tdd.md §12/§13). Pre-fills callback_phone.
_emergency_flag: internal trigger set by flag_emergency tool, read+cleared by before_model_callback (tdd.md §5/§11).

claim_notification_draft: consolidated Slot-Filling state per gecx-design-guide.md 'Feature Encapsulation' -- one sub-object per FNOL slot plus readback_confirmed and internal orchestrator bookkeeping (_last_asked_slot, _awaiting_readback_response).
_system_message: dynamic-prompting directive written by before_model_callback each turn, referenced in instruction.txt as {_system_message} (design guide 'Dynamic Prompting').

_submission_acknowledged: internal one-shot guard set by after_model_callback once the submit_claim_notification outcome has been acknowledged, so the acknowledgment is not re-spoken on later model calls within the same turn or on subsequent turns (see after_model_callback's python_code.py).

variableDeclarations.name: telephony-caller-id
variableDeclarations.description: GTP platform-supplied session parameter containing the caller's ANI (phone number), auto-populated on every call. May be absent/empty if no custom SBC/SIP configuration supplies it (tdd.md §12). Used to pre-fill claim_notification_draft.callback_phone for read-back confirmation instead of a cold ask.

variableDeclarations.name: _emergency_flag
variableDeclarations.description: Internal signal set by the flag_emergency tool the moment the LLM judges the caller's language indicates an active emergency. Read and cleared by before_model_callback, which checks this before anything else (confirmed priority ordering, tdd.md §11). NEVER override in evals.

variableDeclarations.name: claim_notification_draft
variableDeclarations.description: Consolidated Slot-Filling state for the 9 FNOL fields (tdd.md §6/§11/§12). One sub-object per slot tracking {value, status, attempts} (required slots) or {value, status} (optional slots: policy_number, callback_window). Top-level readback_confirmed gates submit_claim_notification visibility. Written by the 9 setter tools and by before_model_callback's orchestrator (_run_slot_filling). NEVER override in evals except via the telephony-caller-id session parameter, which drives the callback_phone pre-fill branch.

variableDeclarations.name: _system_message
variableDeclarations.description: Internal dynamic-prompting directive written each turn by before_model_callback's Slot-Filling orchestrator (_run_slot_filling): the next question to ask, the readback recap, or an error message. Referenced in root_agent's instruction.txt as {_system_message}. NEVER override in evals.

variableDeclarations.name: _submission_acknowledged
variableDeclarations.description: Internal one-shot guard written by after_model_callback ('true'/'false' string) once the submit_claim_notification outcome (success or failure) has been spoken, preventing the acknowledgment from being re-injected on a later model call within the same turn or on subsequent turns during the failure fallback conversation. NEVER override in evals.
